Writing Samples.Student Text (Full): I used to believe that silence meant weakness. I thought the loudest people were the strongest, and that confidence had to be visible to be real. Growing up in a competitive environment, I often felt invisible when I chose to listen rather than speak. But as I grew older, I began to understand that strength can be quiet. The most influential people in my life were not the ones who talked the most; they were the ones who listened deeply, thought carefully, and acted with purpose. Silence, I learned, is not the absence of strength — it is often its most powerful form. [Excerpt – full text in file AEL.docx]

Writing Samples.Assignment / Date:
Argumentative Essay Intro – Draft
3 Feb 2026 – 112 words
Writing Samples.Student Text (Full): In recent years, education has changed significantly due to technological advancements. The most discussed topic today is the comparison between online learning and onsite learning. While onsite learning has been the dominant form of education for many decades, online learning has become increasingly popular, especially after recent global changes that affected education systems worldwide. This shift has raised concerns about the effectiveness of online learning compared to traditional classroom learning. Although online learning offers certain advantages, onsite learning remains the better form of education because it promotes direct interaction, better concentration, and stronger academic engagement.

Instructor-provided model / general feedback text:
Artificial intelligence has rapidly become part of everyday university life. Many students now turn to AI tools to support their writing; organize their ideas; and conduct research more efficiently. Because these technologies are easily accessible and simple to use; their presence in academic work continues to expand. This growing use has sparked an ongoing debate within higher education. Some educators and students question whether relying on artificial intelligence weakens essential academic skills or encourages dishonest practices. Others argue that; when used responsibly; AI can serve as a valuable learning aid that supports understanding rather than replacing effort. To this end; artificial intelligence is neither inherently harmful nor automatically beneficial. Its educational value depends on how it is used. When approached critically and ethically; it can enhance learning; when misused; it risks undermining both intellectual development and academic integrity.
[Instructor note: 'this is mine more formal and academic my dear.']

Writing Samples.Assignment / Date:
First Body Paragraph – Original
15 Feb 2026 – 186 words
Writing Samples.Student Text (Full): One important concern about using Artificial Intelligence in higher education is that students may start depending on it too much. AI is meant to support learning, but when it becomes the main source for doing assignments, it can slowly weaken important skills like critical thinking and problem solving. When students use AI to write essays or answer difficult questions, they miss the process of thinking deeply, questioning ideas, and building their own arguments. Critical thinking becomes weaker because it grows through practice and effort, not by receiving ready answers. In 2023, UNESCO warned that the uncontrolled use of generative AI in education could affect the development of essential cognitive skills. This is especially serious at the university level, where students are expected to analyze information and form independent opinions. From a critical point of view, although AI makes work faster and easier, too much attachment to it can encourage passive learning and make it harder to distinguish between helpful support and cheating. For this reason, AI should be used as a tool to guide learning, not as a replacement for students' own thinking and effort.
Writing Samples.Instructor Feedback (Full):
Fatima GUERCH (16 Feb 2026, 12:48 PM):
«Well-structured and logically sequenced. but use this:
1 – Add one sentence explaining how dependence weakens critical thinking
2 – Dependence and reliance ideas are repeated, use synonyms
3 – critical analysis with cited information»

Writing Samples.Assignment / Date:
Second Body Paragraph – Student Revision (comment id from 24 Feb)
24 Feb 2026, 2:49 AM
Writing Samples.Student Text (Full):
The use of intelligence in universities has a serious problem. Artificial intelligence can affect how honest students are when they do their work. Artificial intelligence tools can help students generate ideas or understand concepts. However when students use intelligence tools in the wrong way it becomes hard to know what work is truly the students work and what is not the students work. If students submit assignments that artificial intelligence completed for them this no longer measures the students real knowledge. The grades of the students may not reflect the effort of the students the skills of the students or the true level of understanding of the students.
Universities are built on honesty, responsibility and real learning. If these principles are ignored the value of a university education decreases. Some scholars at Harvard University explain that assessments are not about testing information but also about building discipline and personal responsibility. When students rely on intelligence to cheat the students lose important opportunities to develop these qualities and to grow academically. Artificial intelligence can be a problem if the students use it to cheat.
In addition if many students misuse intelligence it can create unfairness. Students who follow the rules may feel disadvantaged compared to those students who depend heavily on intelligence tools. This situation can reduce trust between the students. For these reasons universities must establish rules that promote responsible use of artificial intelligence while protecting academic integrity and maintaining high educational standards. Universities need to make sure that artificial intelligence is used in a way that is fair to all the students. Artificial intelligence should be used to help the students learn not to help the students cheat.
Writing Samples.Instructor Feedback (Full):
Fatima GUERCH (7 Mar 2026, 4:20 PM):
«Evaluation: Argument present but explanation and development are limited.
Feedback: Improve evidence or use a stronger one with in-text citation and example to support the claim. Try to expand the reasoning before giving the example. Revise the lesson and structure.»

Writing Samples.Assignment / Date:
Second Body Paragraph – FINAL SUBMISSION
6 Mar 2026, 10:16 PM – 198 words  |  Submission id 6533
Writing Samples.Student Text (Full):
Another major worry is how AI impacts fairness and student relationships. Some students use AI a lot to do their assignments while others do their work on their own it can make school unfair. Students who take time to research write and revise their work might feel discouraged when they see others getting better results with little effort by using AI. This can make classmates not trust each other and make students less motivated to work.
Over time it can also cause problems in the classroom because students start to think that doing well in school is not about trying or being good at something.
For instance a study at Harvard University found that some students who used AI tools in a way got higher grades than their friends who did their work without AI help (Harvard University, 2023). These unfair situations can hurt how well students work together because they might not want to share ideas or help each other much. Also relying much on AI can hurt the development of key skills, like critical thinking solving problems and perseverance. So universities should make rules that guide the responsible use of AI to protect fairness and keep school honest.
Writing Samples.Instructor Feedback (Full):
Student also submitted further revision as comment id 562 (7 Mar 11:02 PM):
Another big worry about intelligence in education is how it affects fairness and relationships between students. When some students use AI tools a lot to do their assignments while others do their work on their own it can make school unfair. Students who take time to research write and revise their work might feel discouraged when they see others getting better results with little effort by using AI. This can make classmates not trust each other and make students less motivated to work. Over time it can also cause problems in the classroom because students start to think that doing well in school is not about trying or being good at something. For instance a study at Harvard University found that some students who used AI tools in a way got higher grades than their friends who did their work without AI help (Harvard University, 2023). These unfair situations can hurt how well students work together because they might not want to share ideas or help each other much. Also relying much on AI can hurt the development of key skills, like critical thinking solving problems and perseverance. So universities should make rules that guide the responsible use of AI to protect fairness and keep school honest.

Writing Samples.Student Text (Full):
1. What writing skills have you learned so far?
I have learned to organize my ideas in paragraphs. I also learned to use connecting words. I have learned to structure an essay, write a clear thesis statement, and organize ideas into body paragraphs. I also learned how to use transition words and write a proper introduction and conclusion.

2. I think I have made good progress in organizing my ideas and writing more clearly. However, I still face some challenges, especially with grammar, vocabulary, and expressing my ideas in an academic way. Sometimes I am not confident about my sentence structure.

3. In the next sessions, I would like to improve my grammar, academic vocabulary, and paraphrasing skills. I also want to learn how to write stronger arguments and avoid common writing mistakes.

Feedback Report CSV.Course: Instructor General Feedback (full text from CSV):

Artificial intelligence has rapidly become part of everyday university life. Many students now turn to AI tools to support their writing; organize their ideas; and conduct research more efficiently. Because these technologies are easily accessible and simple to use; their presence in academic work continues to expand. This growing use has sparked an ongoing debate within higher education. Some educators and students question whether relying on artificial intelligence weakens essential academic skills or encourages dishonest practices. Others argue that; when used responsibly; AI can serve as a valuable learning aid that supports understanding rather than replacing effort. To this end; artificial intelligence is neither inherently harmful nor automatically beneficial. Its educational value depends on how it is used. When approached critically and ethically; it can enhance learning; when misused; it risks undermining both intellectual development and academic integrity.

Instructor note: 'this is mine more formal and academic my dear.'
